{"key_event":"Civil War and Reconstruction state-building","reasoning":"The Civil War, federal taxation, conscription, emancipation, the Freedmen’s Bureau, and Reconstruction amendments markedly increased national coercive, fiscal, and constitutional authority, though Southern resistance kept state reach from being overwhelming. Civil society remained very strong and broadened through abolitionist, Black, and pro-Union mobilization, but national civic cohesion fractured and white paramilitary resistance made the increase only modest.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":8.5,"state_change":1.0,"state_power":7.8}
